title: Tanacetum niveum
intro: Tanacetum niveum, also known as snow tansy, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its white flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, headache, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum niveum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Tanacetum niveum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in aromatherapy and topical applications, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically proven.
taxonomy: Tanacetum niveum is classified in the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes daisies, sunflowers, and asters. Tanacetum niveum is further grouped into the genus Tanacetum and the subfamily Asteroideae. The species Tanacetum niveum is a member of the tribe Anthemideae within the subfamily Asteroideae.
